item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No intentional link, no. It was originally going to be part of its own series of articles, but I ended up never getting them written.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is there any relation between this thing and SCP-3583?

Also, this was amazingly freaky and hopeless. Poor D-99.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just a quick note that this page seems to be missing the links to the prev/next SCP at the bottom of the page. EDIT: My bad, I just noticed that the surrounding SCPs are also missing these links. Do we not do these anymore?

As for content, definitely a +1 from me. I've had enough trips to weird places with a sketchy GPS to relate to this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I got a really strong Corbenic vibe—Project Galahad from that place. I wish there would be some connection somehow.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved imagining the creepy cultist-speak in that standard, blandly cheerful GPS voiceover. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I really like this one. That narration by the D-class is fantastic. I think you absolutely nailed that tone of angry disbelief that so many of us would probably have in such an insane situation.

+1, obviously.

Edit: damn, I guess I already rated it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. Despite it being the millionth article about people disappearing to a spooky hell dimension, it's well written enough to justify it. The contrast between the mundane satnav and bizarre happenings was what pushed me to +1 though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gotta give D-99 his props; not sure I'd remain calm enough to keep reporting like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wrote it based on how I (English) would react in that situation ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Surreal and darkly comical. A little sweary, but I'm not sure I'd react any differently given the circumstances. Good show.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gotta say, if I hadn't already read a bunch of really creepy travel-related skips on this wiki, this would probably be amazing and worth a +1.

That said, I'm still going to +1 it because it's quite enjoyable, just not really anything I haven't seen before.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A death cult rigging up a GPS to sacrifice people is a cool concept, and this is a solid execution of it. My only gripe is that the cult-speak messages began to feel repetitive and that D-99 was repeating what he saw too much, but other than that this was a fun read.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just your everyday, normal GPS, With a completely normal, everyday purpose. Nothing going on here at all. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Make your Genre Savvy roll.

"Nothing good ever happens in a desert temple during a full eclipse."

Ooh, natural 20!

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Been sitting on this one for a while, because I was originally planning on releasing it as a series of 3 or 4 different articles. At least one of those might end up being a thing in the future, but for now I just decided it was time to post this one.

Thanks to the following for reading the draft:
SunnyClockwork
DrBleep
Waterfire
Modern_Erasmus
ARD
Weryllium

You can check out more of my stuff here!

The image was completely hand-made by me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember reading this draft waaayyyyy back in July. I loved it then, and I love it now, excellent use of dialogue and scenery to display one mans trip into hell via GPS. +1 heartily.
